div.h2: struct file_operations — Memory Layout and VFS Dispatch
div.p: Each field is a function pointer (8 bytes on x86-64). VFS checks for NULL before dispatch.

g.text: struct file_operations [sizeof ≈ 280 bytes on x86-64, 35 pointers × 8 B]

div.p: Cache Line Note Function pointers shown: 8 fields × 8 B = 64 B Cache line 0 (0x00–0x3F): owner, llseek, read, write, read_iter, write_iter, iterate_shared, poll Cache line 1 (0x40–0x7F): unlocked_ioctl, compat_ioctl, mmap, open, flush, release, fsync, fasync Total struct: ≈ 280 B (35 pointers) = ~5 cache lines Hot path fields (open/read/write/release) reside in lines 0–1 → spatial locality on repeated dispatch

g.text: offset 0x08 │ .llseek │ 8 B │ fn ptr │ = NULL → default_llseek
offset 0x38 │ .poll │ 8 B │ fn ptr │ = NULL → always-ready [M2/M3] / mydevice_poll [M4]
offset 0x40 │.unlocked_ioctl│ 8 B │ fn ptr │ = NULL → -ENOTTY [M2] / mydevice_ioctl [M3]
